Where are the, myriads Of wild
duck which Is or 20 years ago dark
ened the sky in migratory flight?


Tens OF thousands have gone to
the bag Of the market hunter-the
fellow who slaughters birds at rest
from a sneak boat; other thousands
have gone to the ssportsman's IL
|gauge-sportsman IL he followed
the code in getting his bag.
| But great as has been the slaugh-
ter. It. has not thinned the flocks
sufficiently to account for the dis
appearance Of the lordly canvas
back, the toothsome mallard, or
that aerial lightning express, the
blue wing teal.


In many sections that mud wal
lowing water hog, the carp, has
done more to chase away the duck
than all the powder that has been
burned.


The greatest crime on the calen-
dar against American sportsmen
was the Introduction of the carp
into our fresh water bodies. / think
this pest was introduced at the time
of the worlds fair, in 1893. They
may have made their appearance
prior to this, but my information is
that some weisenheimer thought it
fine to show the carp, with other
things, when we were honoring
Chris Columb.


| All l have to say with respect to
this individual is that / don't wish
him any more harm than to be turn
ed loose in a camp of duck hunters
and fishermen some dismal after
noon. If you ever cast a fly or
artificial bait, or crouched in
blind you know what this implies.
All the way from the breeding
ground, in the north, to the waters
in the south, the way of old was
dotted with wild rice, and the duck
fed as they flew, with never a
thought of hunger. But then came
the carp, and bingO went the rice
and with it the duck.


The carp, wallowing in the mud,
devoured the wild rice roots until
today, wherever the carp roots, not
spear of wild rice grows.
Efforts to propagate rice are be-

ties where carp is not.
1t is to these men give this in
formation on growing rice, for /
know it will be appreciated.
Wild rice will grow in fresh
water south of So degrees north
latitude. The difficulty to over
come is poor termination. II per-
mitted to dry, the seed will not ger
inmate.


1t is best to sow in the spring.
Mud bottom is necessary and the
water must not flow too swiftly,
nor must it be stagnant.


Seed must be gathered as soon
as mature and shipped In burlap
sacks to cold storage rooms. The
shortest possible time must elapse
between harvesting the seed and
storing it. rtr

plant, while fresh and before fer
mentation, it must be put into
buckets, open barrels or watts, COW
